prompt:
A 3D printed PLA fincan for a 35 mm model rocket stands upright on a workbench, its cylindrical base blending into four evenly spaced fins with clean, sharp trailing edges. Rectangular cutouts on the sides reveal mounting bays for 9 g servos, and shallow channels are visible along the surface for routing servo wires up toward the body tube. The internal diameter is clearly sized for a 35 mm airframe, and the motor mount opening at the base hints at standard hobby motor compatibility.

The environment is a near-future solarpunk rocketry workspace with soft, even lighting, a small FDM printer and a few servo motors in the blurred background, and organized bins of hardware and wires. The 512×512 image is photorealistic with gentle stylization, no logos or text on the fincan or tools, and a composition that centers the servo cutouts, wiring channels, and aerodynamic fin shape while the rest of the bench remains softly out of focus, emphasizing a guidance-ready fincan.
